response: The 2007 documentary "Zoo" was more successful than the 1998 documentary "Divine Trash" based on its recognition at the Sundance Film Festival, where it won one of 16 awards out of 856 candidates. "Zoo" was selected as one of the top five American films to be presented at the Directors' Fortnight sidebar at the 2007 Cannes Film Festival, indicating its prominence in the documentary film scene. In contrast, "Divine Trash" is a 1998 documentary directed by Steve Yeager about John Waters, but no specific success metrics or awards are provided for it in the context. Therefore, based on the information provided, "Zoo" was more successful than "Divine Trash."
